title: A Flesh-Eating Parasite Just Crossed Into the US For the First Time in Decades
script: A flesh-eating parasite that devours animals alive just crossed into the U.S. for the first time in decades. The USDA confirmed a New World screwworm — a fly whose larvae burrow into living flesh — in a Texas calf last week. Screwworms were eradicated from the U.S. back in 1966. Now officials are racing to stop the comeback, releasing 4 million sterile flies per week near the border. The food supply is safe. Livestock is not.
